task_signature.context: GA4 naming convention for FitMe. Rule established 2026-04-08. Used by /analytics spec to generate compliant event names and by /analytics validate to audit existing events.

learned_patterns.pattern: Screen-prefix naming rule — all screen-scoped events
learned_patterns.decision: Every event tied to a specific screen MUST start with that screen's prefix. Pattern: {screen}_{action_noun}. The screen should be identifiable from the event name alone without reading source code.
source_executions: CLAUDE.md Analytics Naming Convention (established 2026-04-08, OQ-9 in home-today-screen v2-audit-report.md)

learned_patterns.pattern: GA4 recommended events — keep dictated names, no prefix
learned_patterns.decision: GA4 recommended events MUST keep their exact GA4 names even though they are tied to specific screens. Adding screen prefix breaks GA4 automatic dashboards. These are exempt from the screen-prefix rule.

ga4_recommended_exempt.login: Auth/Sign In screen — GA4 dictated, unprefixed
ga4_recommended_exempt.sign_up: Sign Up screen — GA4 dictated, unprefixed. Mark as conversion.
ga4_recommended_exempt.tutorial_complete: Onboarding Final Step — GA4 dictated. Mark as conversion.

learned_patterns.decision: Events that span screens or are global session-level signals stay unprefixed. These are app_open, session_start, sign_in, sign_up. Do NOT add home_ or training_ prefix to these.
source_executions: CLAUDE.md Analytics rule — What this rule does NOT cover

learned_patterns.decision: Standard parameter patterns by event category derived from analytics-taxonomy.csv.
learned_patterns.units_rule: Weight always in kg (SI). Duration always in seconds (SI). No unit suffixes in parameter names.

learned_patterns.decision: Mark these events as conversions in GA4: sign_up, tutorial_complete, workout_complete, meal_log, cross_feature_engagement. Conversions map to the PRD's primary metric or kill criteria.
source_executions: analytics-taxonomy.csv Conversion column: yes on sign_up, tutorial_complete, workout_complete, meal_log, cross_feature_engagement

learned_patterns.decision: consent_granted and consent_denied are always logged regardless of consent state (they are the consent signal itself). All other events are consent-gated. Analytics tests must verify: (1) event fires on action, (2) event does NOT fire when consent denied (except consent_* events).

learned_patterns.pattern: Training v2 analytics spec — 12 training_* events
learned_patterns.decision: Training v2 (PR #74) shipped 12 analytics events, all training_* prefixed: training_session_viewed, training_exercise_started, training_exercise_completed, training_set_logged, training_set_copied, training_weight_changed, training_rest_timer_started, + 5 more. Per-exercise granularity at weekly/30d/90d cohorts. Kill criteria: per-exercise event drop-off > 15% vs training_workout_start.

CSV columns: Category, Event Name, GA4 Type, Screen/Trigger, Parameter 1-5, Conversion, Scope, Notes. screen_scope column values: screen name (home/nutrition/training/stats/settings/onboarding/auth) or 'global'. Every new event added to taxonomy CSV in same commit as the enum declaration in Swift.
source_executions: home-today-screen T7 (analytics enum + CSV rows in same task)

anti_patterns.pattern: Event name without screen prefix for screen-scoped events
anti_patterns.what_went_wrong: Pre-2026-04-08 events like workout_start, exercise_log, stats_view exist in the taxonomy without screen prefix. These are grandfathered — they will be renamed via /analytics validate with GA4 event aliases to preserve historical dashboards. ANY new event added after 2026-04-08 that is screen-scoped MUST have the prefix or it blocks PRD approval.
anti_patterns.source_execution: analytics-taxonomy.csv rows 13-16 (workout_* pre-rule), CLAUDE.md Backwards compatibility note

anti_patterns.pattern: Adding screen prefix to GA4 recommended events
anti_patterns.what_went_wrong: Writing 'auth_login' or 'onboarding_tutorial_begin' breaks GA4 automatic dashboards that depend on the exact recommended event names. Use 'login' and 'tutorial_begin' as-is.

anti_patterns.pattern: Weight in pounds or duration in minutes
anti_patterns.what_went_wrong: Weight parameters must be float kg (SI). Duration parameters must be int seconds (SI). No unit suffixes. Mixed units break cross-cohort analysis.
anti_patterns.source_execution: analytics-taxonomy.csv notes: 'Weight always in kg (SI). No unit suffix.'

speedup_instructions: When /analytics spec is invoked: (1) check event name against prefix_map — if screen-scoped, enforce prefix; (2) check if event is GA4 recommended — if so, keep dictated name; (3) use parameter_patterns for standard parameter sets; (4) add row to analytics-taxonomy.csv in same commit. When /analytics validate is invoked: compare all existing events against this prefix map, flag any screen-scoped event missing its prefix, generate migration plan with GA4 event aliases for legacy events.
